code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test26-OCT-2018Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment or dehydration. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria or infection markers. These findings are concerning for renal dysfunction, possibly with tubular involvement (e.g., diabetic nephropathy or other glomerular/tubular pathology). Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
